entries.title: OverKill Hill P³™ — Precision · Protocol · Promptcraft
entries.description: OverKill Hill P³™ is the digital forge where custom GPT systems are architected, tempered and polished for real-world performance.
headings: The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar
entries.body: The Forge Precision · Protocol · Promptcraft The digital forge This is where custom GPT systems are architected, tempered and polished for real-world performance — with the same care you’d give to a structural beam or a bridge schematic. ⚙ Forge in motion — actively iterated, not under construction OverKill Hill runs in forge mode, not museum mode. Protocols are tempered on the bench in the open, documentation is versioned mid‑draft, and new conduits get routed before the cosmetic conduit covers go on. You’re walking the shop floor between heats — mind the sparks, and feel free to look over the plans. Explore the projects Read the manifesto What happens at the Hill? OverKill Hill is where prompts aren’t just “clever questions” — they’re protocols . We prototype, stress-test and tune GPT systems so they can handle messy reality: human context, ambiguity, unknowns and real stakes. Fresh from the Forge The most recent thing to clear the workbench and go public. Fresh from the Forge The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar What happens when diagrams get cheap enough to be wrong on purpose? ROY — Return on Your Words — is the metric. A Council of AIs ran eight platforms against the same…

entries.title: About OverKill Hill P³™ — Precision, Protocol & Promptcraft
entries.description: Learn about OverKill Hill P³™ — the mind, philosophy, and approach behind our protocol-driven custom GPT systems and AI solutions for real-world complexity.
entries.body: The Forge About OverKill Hill P³™ OverKill Hill P³™ is where design, precision, and automation converge — between the idea and the system that makes it real, between “we should automate this” and “we trust what we’ve built together.” Explore the projects Back to the forge Who’s on the Hill At OverKill Hill P³™, we specialize in crafting custom GPT systems designed to tackle complex, real-world challenges. Our approach combines deep industry knowledge with cutting-edge AI technology to deliver solutions that are not only innovative but also practical and reliable. Founded by Jamie Hill — Lead Enterprise Architect with 13+ years of enterprise experience across complex systems, Salesforce architecture, and AI strategy. A career built on solving high-stakes problems at scale: from deep operational complexity into intelligent automation. That depth of real-world implementation experience is the defining differentiator that makes OverKill Hill P³™ systems grounded in real-world stakes, not just technical possibility. We understand that every business is unique, and we pride ourselves on our ability to listen, learn, and adapt our solutions to fit the distinct challenges faced by each…

entries.title: OverKill Hill P³™ — Contact

entries.body: The Forge How to Reach The Hill Our mascot, The OKHP³™ MurderBird, isn't a passenger pigeon, so don't expect it to deliver your message. But don't be shy — despite the menacing metallic appearance, we really do want to hear from you! Contact For project inquiries, collaboration opportunities, or professional engagements, reach out directly: Email: contact@overkillhill.com Location: Operating remotely across U.S. Central Time (UTC‑6) For updates, news, and musings from the forge, follow OverKill Hill P³™ on social media or subscribe to our newsletter. We look forward to connecting with you! Back to the forge Support the Forge with Ko‑fi OverKill Hill P³™ started with the feeling that if something is worth doing, it’s worth overdoing! To explore every branch and turn of the rabbit-hole. We share the results of that exploration, the protocol-driven promptcraft, and the systems we build along the way. Ko‑fi is a creator support platform that lets people send one‑time tips or small donations using trusted payment providers like PayPal and Stripe. Your payment details don’t pass through this site — they’re handled directly by those processors, the same way they are on many online shops.…

entries.title: OverKill Hill P³™ — The Manifesto
entries.description: The OverKill Hill P³ manifesto: rigor over vibes, prompts as protocols, and building AI systems that withstand real-world complexity.
headings: 01. Overkill is about responsibility, not complexity
04. Tools should grow with the humans using them
05. Everything is an invitation to refine
entries.body: The Forge Manifesto of OKHP³™ A hill is something you choose to stand on. OverKill Hill P³™ is ours — the place where we stake out what we believe about systems, prompts and the humans using them. Explore the projects Back to the forge Rigor over vibes “If you’re going to let a system make or shape real decisions, you owe it the same level of design rigor as a physical structure. No vibes-only scaffolding.” 01. Overkill is about responsibility, not complexity OverKill doesn’t mean over-complicating things. It means going deep enough into the layers that the people using the system can safely lean on it, even when they can’t see every bolt. 02. Prompts are protocols We don’t treat prompts like spells you whisper to a model. We treat them as protocols: structured, documented, testable. Inputs, outputs, failure modes, expectations — all explicit. 03. Ambiguity deserves design, not blame Real life is messy. People don’t always know exactly what they’re asking for. Our job is to design GPT systems that can hold space for that ambiguity without collapsing or making users feel foolish. 04. Tools should grow with the humans using them We build systems that are teachable and extensible.…

entries.body: Precision · Protocol · Promptcraft | ROY · Mermaid · Council of AIs | Article v0.3 — Visual Edition The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar A picture is not automatically worth 1,000 words. The real metric is ROY — Return on Your Words : the clarity, compression, and shared understanding extracted per word invested. This is the v0.3 cut — what happened when one LinkedIn post became a Mermaid competition, a Council of AIs, and a better theory of visual communication. Jamie Hill OverKill Hill P³™ April 2026 ~18 min read 🚀 v0.3 — Visual Edition Live The deck is public, the polls are open, and the scoring bracket is running. v0.3 adds the Etch-AI-Sketch slide deck (18 slides, square and wide formats), the public scoring bracket, the poll schedule (April 22–30), and the three-lane scoring model. Council interviews and deeper workflow essays are still coming in future updates. I started drawing on a computer in the early 1990s with AutoCAD R10 . Back then it was black screens, command lines, coordinates instead of cursors. If you wanted to express an idea visually, you did not drag shapes around. You constructed it, line by line, with the same kind of concentration you would use writing a…

entries.title: Launch Artifacts — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Because this began as a LinkedIn experiment, the launch assets matter. The article is the canonical home. The post is the spear. The first comment is the afterburner.
entries.body: Because this began as a LinkedIn experiment, the launch assets matter. The article is the canonical home. The post is the spear. The first comment is the afterburner. LinkedIn Article — v0.1 The original published piece. The canonical home of the thesis, the council run, and both diagrams. View article v0.1 copy The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar I’ve been drawing pictures on computers since the early 1990s. Back then, it was AutoCAD R10: black screens, command lines, coordinates instead of cursors. If you wanted to express an idea visually, you did not drag shapes around. You constructed it, line by line, with the same kind of concentration you would use writing a careful spec. That early experience baked in a bias I still carry. A diagram is not decoration.

entries.title: Attention Is Finite (and Text Hides Flow) — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: People do not fail to read because they are lazy. They fail to read because their capacity to absorb meaning is finite and the workday is a relentless attention shredder.
entries.body: People do not fail to read because they are lazy. They fail to read because their capacity to absorb meaning is finite and the workday is a relentless attention shredder. Every meeting, every notification, every summary that is really just a longer version of the thing it was supposed to replace — they all draw from the same account. By the time someone reaches your explanation, they may already be overdrawn. Text handles nuance and precision well. What it handles poorly is flow. A paragraph can describe a process sequentially, but the reader has to do all the structural work themselves — building the mental model one sentence at a time, inferring the branches, guessing where the loops are, deciding which steps are parallel and which are dependent.

entries.title: Contender Pre-Reads — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Contender Pre-Reads The deck includes my first-pass read on each platform. These are not final audience results. They are the starting layer before public polling and Council-assisted rubric review.
entries.body: Contender Pre-Reads The deck includes my first-pass read on each platform. These are not final audience results. They are the starting layer before public polling and Council-assisted rubric review. V1 Reads Copilot V1 — Presentation-ready system thinking; clear phases and deliverable feel. Claude V1 — Elegant storyteller; strong framing, maybe too polished. Perplexity V1 — Pragmatic, grounded, business-first utility. ChatGPT V1 — Clarity, hierarchy, strong loop logic, a little swagger. Gemini V1 — Restrained synthesis; simplifies without flattening. Notion V1 — Quiet, minimalist, useful; shows the shape without theater. Replit V1 — Builder’s eye; asks whether it ships, renders, and survives the IDE.

entries.title: The Council of AIs — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: What I was running, without naming it at first, was not automation and not a pipeline. It was a deliberative body. A council is the body. The counsel is what it produces.
entries.body: What I was running, without naming it at first, was not automation and not a pipeline. It was a deliberative body. A council is the body. The counsel is what it produces. That distinction mattered because the value was never going to come from one model saying one brilliant thing once. The value came from parallel generation, comparative judgment, and hybrid synthesis. Council roles that emerged in practice Same brief. Different strengths. Better synthesis. ChatGPT Strongest all-around scaffolding for article, post, and comment synthesis. Claude Best narrative architecture and the most honest second-round diagram. Copilot Cleanest renderer-level Mermaid configuration and strongest V1 discipline. Perplexity Tightest ROY framing and disciplined explanation of the visual ROI thesis.

entries.title: Ideation to Evaluation: You Judge — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Ideation to Evaluation: You Judge The slide deck is now live, and that changes the status of the argument. Up to this point, this article has been doing the work in prose.
entries.body: Ideation to Evaluation: You Judge The slide deck is now live, and that changes the status of the argument. Up to this point, this article has been doing the work in prose. v0.3 is the moment the experiment leaves prose-only mode and becomes a visible field for comparison, judgment, and public scoring. The Council produced the diagrams. The deck now puts them side by side. What had previously been a claim about diagramming can now be inspected, compared, and judged in public. That shift matters because the whole ROY argument has always been about whether a visual earns the words that produced it. If a diagram cannot create enough clarity to justify the explanatory cost, it does not matter how polished it looks. It is still overhead.

entries.title: Mermaid the Syntax vs Mermaid.ai the Platform — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Mermaid the syntax is a language. Mermaid.ai the platform is one AI-assisted on-ramp to that language. Those are not the same thing. Mermaid.ai is a generative AI product purpose-built to help you produce Mermaid code…
entries.body: Mermaid the syntax is a language. Mermaid.ai the platform is one AI-assisted on-ramp to that language. Those are not the same thing. Mermaid.ai is a generative AI product purpose-built to help you produce Mermaid code faster. ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Copilot, and a growing list of extensions can all generate Mermaid syntax on request. None of them invented the format. They accelerate authorship of it. The Council of AIs comparison wasn’t testing which AI could generate the prettiest picture. It was testing which AI produced the most accurate, most maintainable, most structurally sound Mermaid code from an evolving brief. The diagram is the visible output. The code is the work product. The AI is the author under evaluation. A Mermaid diagram is not AI. It is text.

entries.title: Not Just Diagramming — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Not Just Diagramming What this field test actually measured: Taste under constraints Honesty in showing nonlinear thought Turning words into structure without losing meaning The difference between…
entries.body: Not Just Diagramming What this field test actually measured: Taste under constraints Honesty in showing nonlinear thought Turning words into structure without losing meaning The difference between “generated” and “designed”

entries.title: The One That Should Have Won — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Mermaid.ai was the obvious ringer. Its entire reason for existing is to produce exactly what I was asking every other council member to produce.
entries.body: Mermaid.ai was the obvious ringer. Its entire reason for existing is to produce exactly what I was asking every other council member to produce. If any platform should have dominated a Mermaid diagram shootout, it’s the one with “Mermaid” in the name. It was the biggest failure of the entire test. Not because it can’t draw. Because it couldn’t wait. The failure has a name: premature rendering . The moment Mermaid.ai detected diagram intent, it executed. No hold state. No absorption phase. I was still building context across multiple prompt turns: narrative first, system logic second, relationship mapping third. By the time I’d finished, Mermaid.ai had already produced three diagrams from the first two sentences. They weren’t wrong. They were premature.

entries.title: From Drawing to Modeling — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: About eight months ago, ChatGPT coughed up a visual I didn’t ask for. It wasn’t an image. It wasn’t a screenshot. It was code . It was a text-based diagram. A flowchart.
entries.body: About eight months ago, ChatGPT coughed up a visual I didn’t ask for. It wasn’t an image. It wasn’t a screenshot. It was code . It was a text-based diagram. A flowchart. A skeleton of boxes and arrows that looked like it had been drawn in Visio but rendered in monospace. I asked the only reasonable question: What the hell is that? It replied: A Mermaid diagram. I asked Copilot to generate one too. Then I discovered Microsoft Loop could render Mermaid natively. Suddenly I wasn’t “drawing” in the old sense anymore. I was describing structure in words and letting the rendering happen downstream. That changed the economics. Mermaid plus LLMs turned diagramming from click-drag labor into text-native iteration.

entries.title: Prompts in the Wild — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Velocity, iteration, and vibe over premature prompt perfection One of the points of this experiment is that the prompts were not highly polished. They were not lab-grade. They were not deeply pre-scripted.
entries.body: Velocity, iteration, and vibe over premature prompt perfection One of the points of this experiment is that the prompts were not highly polished. They were not lab-grade. They were not deeply pre-scripted. They were not engineered like final-form production assets. Most of them started as live dictation — quick, directional, natural-language prompts spoken in the moment while the idea was still moving. That matters. If every prompt had to be honed, polished, and weaponized before I could use it, the process would lose much of what made it valuable in the first place. The friction would rise. The mental cost would rise. The velocity would drop. And at some point I may as well go back to a traditional tool and manually draw the thing in Visio.

entries.title: Round 1 — The Clean Lie — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: To test the thesis, I ran the same brief through a Council of AIs : ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, Gemini, Perplexity, Notion, and Replit. Same question. Same deliverables.
entries.body: To test the thesis, I ran the same brief through a Council of AIs : ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, Gemini, Perplexity, Notion, and Replit. Same question. Same deliverables. Same demand: write the post, write the article, and diagram the actual ideation process behind them. The first round showed exactly what I suspected. Most V1 diagrams were too tidy. Too linear. Too willing to perform certainty. The best of that first wave came from Copilot because it was the cleanest architecturally and the only one configuring Mermaid at the renderer level with YAML front matter.

entries.title: Round 2 — Honestly Messy — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Once every model had seen the others, the second round got better fast. The diagrams became less eager to perform certainty and more willing to show recursion.
entries.body: Once every model had seen the others, the second round got better fast. The diagrams became less eager to perform certainty and more willing to show recursion. That is why Claude’s V2 won the second round: it made the loops visible. Ignition. Strategy. Craft. Proof. Ship. Revision arrows threading back through all of them. That shape is the thesis. ⚙️ Council V2 — Claude Synthesis (Round 2 Winner) Open in Mermaid ↗ See launch artifacts ↓ View live Mermaid render ↓ --- config: theme: neutral fontFamily: Segoe UI, Inter, Arial, sans-serif themeVariables: primaryColor: '#EAF2FF' primaryTextColor: '#14213D' primaryBorderColor: '#325DCC' lineColor: '#64748B' secondaryColor: '#FFF7E8' tertiaryColor: '#EEFDF3' edgeLabelBackground: '#FFFFFF' clusterBkg: '#F8FAFC' clusterBorder: '#CBD5E0'…

entries.title: ROY: Return on Your Words — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Once the rendering cost collapsed, the real question became unavoidable: What did the words actually buy? The ROY Formula Clarity Delivered ÷ Words Required to Deliver It Return on Your Words = understanding extracted ÷…
entries.body: Once the rendering cost collapsed, the real question became unavoidable: What did the words actually buy? The ROY Formula Clarity Delivered ÷ Words Required to Deliver It Return on Your Words = understanding extracted ÷ words invested A 47-bullet Slack message? Poor ROY. A “quick summary” that still requires a meeting to decode? Negative ROY. A diagram generated from 50 well-chosen words that a team grasps in five seconds and uses correctly? Potentially extraordinary ROY. That is why I don’t believe a picture is automatically worth 1,000 words. Some pictures are decorative wallpaper with a superiority complex. Some diagrams are just confusion with borders and connector lines.

entries.title: Scoring Bracket — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Scoring Brackets V1 Heat A: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity V1 Heat B: Copilot, Notion, Replit V2 Heat A: ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, Notion V2 Heat B: ChatGPT Pro, Claude, Replit, Perplexity The audience vote will…
entries.body: Scoring Brackets V1 Heat A: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity V1 Heat B: Copilot, Notion, Replit V2 Heat A: ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, Notion V2 Heat B: ChatGPT Pro, Claude, Replit, Perplexity The audience vote will not be treated as the only answer. It will be treated as one signal. After the polls close, the scoring breakdown will compare three lanes: Audience scoring — what communicated best to human viewers Architect scoring — judgment against fidelity, clarity, structure, and usefulness Council-assisted scoring — model-assisted rubric review across the full field The gap between those scores is probably where the interesting lesson lives.

entries.body: Scoring Model The final v0.3.x debrief will compare three scoring methodologies: Audience Scoring What communicated best to human viewers across LinkedIn polls. Architect Scoring Evaluated against: First-glance comprehension Semantic fidelity Diagram structure Visual hierarchy Theming discipline Iteration improvement (V1→V2) Usefulness as thinking artifact Council-Assisted Scoring Each AI evaluated the others’ outputs using the same rubric.

entries.title: The Thesis, Standing on Its Own — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: A picture can absolutely be worth more than the words that prompted it into existence. Not because the words disappeared. Not because reading stopped mattering.
entries.body: A picture can absolutely be worth more than the words that prompted it into existence. Not because the words disappeared. Not because reading stopped mattering. But because the words were invested in a form that improved assimilation. That is the work I keep circling back to at OverKill Hill P³™ : words → structure → systems → leverage . The tools will keep changing. The interfaces will keep changing. The models will keep changing. The question underneath them is not going away. When is meaning best delivered as words — and when is it better delivered as the visual shape those words can bring into existence? This page is not the last version. It is the first version that has earned its permanence.

entries.title: V1 First-Pass Diagrams — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: V1 First-Pass Diagrams All seven models responded to the same brief. These are the first-pass diagrams before critique, constraint, or revision. Copilot V1 Presentation-ready system thinking.
entries.body: V1 First-Pass Diagrams All seven models responded to the same brief. These are the first-pass diagrams before critique, constraint, or revision. Copilot V1 Presentation-ready system thinking. Strong architecture, clear phases, deliverable feel. View on Mermaid.ai → Claude V1 Elegant storyteller — occasionally too polished. Narrative intelligence, strong framing. View on Mermaid.ai → ChatGPT V1 Clarity, hierarchy, and a little swagger. Balanced composition, strong loops, polish and branding. View on Mermaid.ai → Perplexity V1 Less theater, more utility. Pragmatic structure, ROI thinking, business framing. View on Mermaid.ai → Gemini V1 Elegance from insight — or just restraint? Synthesis and reduction, simplifies without flattening.

entries.title: V2 Optimized Diagrams — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: V2 Optimized Diagrams After critique, constraint, and more context. V2 is not first instinct — V2 is adaptation. Claude V2 Thinks beautifully.
entries.body: V2 Optimized Diagrams After critique, constraint, and more context. V2 is not first instinct — V2 is adaptation. Claude V2 Thinks beautifully. Earns the elegance through honest recursion rather than decorating it on top. View on Mermaid.ai → Copilot V2 Architecture preserved. Emotion and discipline. Boardroom-ready if flair does not damage structure. View on Mermaid.ai → ChatGPT V2 Denser reasoning. Explicit loop logic with brand and mechanics. More deliberate, less merely clever. View on Mermaid.ai → ChatGPT V2 Pro Art of the possible — feature-rich and theatrical. Pushes Mermaid further. Diagram or poster? View on Mermaid.ai → Perplexity V2 Can pragmatism also become beautiful? Visually memorable, clarity under complexity.

entries.title: What a Mermaid Diagram Actually Is — The First Diagram Is Usually a Liar | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Mermaid is not AI. That sentence needs to land before anything else in this article makes sense, because if you carry the wrong assumption into the council comparison, the entire experiment looks like something it…
entries.body: Mermaid is not AI. That sentence needs to land before anything else in this article makes sense, because if you carry the wrong assumption into the council comparison, the entire experiment looks like something it isn’t. A Mermaid diagram is text. Structured syntax. A specific grammar that a JavaScript library reads and renders into a visual diagram inside a browser. When I say I put AI platforms through the same diagramming exercise, I’m not talking about image generation. I’m not talking about a model painting a flowchart. I’m talking about plain text that follows rules, and the diagram that appears on screen is what that text decided to be. The closest analogy is an SVG file. An SVG isn’t a photograph. It isn’t a rendered image.

entries.title: Our Projects | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Explore the innovative projects and systems crafted at the OverKill Hill P³™ forge — from custom GPT systems to AI tools for real-world complexity.
entries.body: Projects from the Hill A sampling of the systems currently being forged at The OverKill Hill P³™ — some public-facing, some internal client work, all built with the same protocol-driven mindset. Glee-fully Personalizable Tools A whole-life ecosystem of custom GPTs focused on emotional design, gentle structure and playful metaphors. Visit Glee-fully → AskJamie A mid-century modern AI helpdesk with a carefully crafted tone: friendly, technically literate and unhurried in all the right ways. Open AskJamie → Protocol Libraries Modular prompt protocols designed for reuse across industries — sales, ops, L&D, creative, and more. Request access → Mermaid Theme Builder A free, visual Mermaid.js theme builder. Pick colors, watch diagrams update live, and export production-ready CSS variables — no prompting required. View the project →

entries.title: BFS Framing Intelligent Futures | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: BFS Framing Intelligent Futures — an OverKill Hill P³™ incubator project that evolved into the AskJamie™ BrandGuard™ custom GPT lens for Builders FirstSource.
headings: From OverKill Hill experiment to BrandGuard™ lens
How it evolved into AskJamie™ BrandGuard™
Why this matters for the next era of digital commerce
From search results to model answers
What the BFS project can do today
New builder, clean break from old employer
“Who is BFS?” for homeowners and pros
Field math: board‑feet, weight, and loads
AskJamie™ BrandGuard™ & the OverKill Hill P³ universe
BrandGuard™ — protecting stories in the model era
OverKill Hill P³ — People, Protocols, Prototypes
For other brands staring at the same horizon
entries.body: OverKill Hill P³ · Incubator Project · Evolved into AskJamie™ BrandGuard™ BFS Framing Intelligent Futures A live, working experiment with Builders FirstSource that answers a hard question: who tells your story inside large language models when they become the default interface? Originally built as an OverKill Hill P³ project, BFS Framing Intelligent Futures became GPT‑BFS01 — the first AskJamie™ BrandGuard™ lens. It turns public BFS knowledge into a conversational expert for wood‑frame construction, and quietly routes every meaningful path back to BLDR.com. Explore BrandGuard™ for your brand Prototype only · Uses public information about Builders FirstSource · Not an official corporate product. For official details, go to BLDR.com . Open GPT‑BFS01 in ChatGPT From OverKill Hill experiment to BrandGuard™ lens This project started as a very personal OverKill Hill P³ question: “If BFS is our house, who’s protecting it in the AI era?” The creator has spent 13 years at Builders FirstSource. Their spouse, daughter, and other close family members also work there. BFS isn’t just an employer; it’s the economic house the family lives in. BFS Framing Intelligent Futures was the incubator…

entries.title: Mermaid Theme Builder | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: A free, visual Mermaid.js theme builder from OverKill Hill P³. Pick colors, preview diagrams live, and export production-ready CSS variables — no prompting, no guessing.
headings: What you get here that you don't get from prompting an LLM
entries.body: Community Tool Open Source V0.3 Public Alpha Mermaid Theme Builder Visual. Instant. Yours. A free, open-source visual editor for Mermaid.js themes. Pick your colors, watch diagrams update live, and export production-ready CSS — no prompting, no guessing, no iteration cycles. Use the Tool Now View Source on GitHub Live Tool · okhp3.github.io ↺ Reload ⤢ Full Screen ⤢ Open Mermaid Theme Builder Full Screen Loading Mermaid Theme Builder… Taking longer than expected? Open the tool in a new tab → Embedded from okhp3.github.io/mermaid-theme-builder — open in a new tab for the best experience Why This Exists What you get here that you don't get from prompting an LLM ✗ Prompting an LLM Generates CSS variable blobs you paste and re-prompt until something looks right. No live preview, no iteration feedback, result varies by model. ✓ Mermaid Theme Builder Pick a color, see every diagram type update instantly. When it looks right, export. One round trip instead of five. This is a community contribution to the Mermaid.js ecosystem — not a commercial product, not a SaaS, not a competitor to Mermaid.ai. It runs entirely in your browser. No account. No data collected. Features What the builder…

entries.title: Found‑Rᵧ | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Found‑Rᵧ is a meta‑framework dedicated to creating tools that can assemble and evolve other GPTs. Explore recursive AI agent architecture and protocol-driven power prompts.
entries.body: Found‑Rᵧ GPTs that build GPTs: the recursive heart of our ecosystem. About Found‑Rᵧ Found‑Rᵧ is a meta‑framework dedicated to creating tools that can assemble and evolve other GPTs. By treating each agent as a component with inputs and outputs, we can chain behaviors, coordinate tasks and generate new capabilities on demand. Underlying GPTs Protocol Compiler: Turns structured specifications into working prompt protocols. Agent Builder: Wraps multiple GPTs into a single orchestrated agent with memory and context management. Recursion Monitor: Watches chains of GPT calls and suggests optimization or termination strategies. Emergent Designer: Experiments with novel configurations and reports on their behaviors.

entries.title: Abrahamic Reference Engine | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: The Abrahamic Reference Engine is a multi-faith concordance linking texts and traditions of Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.
entries.body: Abrahamic Reference Engine A semantic index across the texts and traditions of Judaism, Christianity and Islam. Work in Progress The Abrahamic Reference Engine is an early experiment in building a multi‑faith concordance. It aims to link names, places, concepts and narratives across the Torah, Bible and Quran, highlighting common ground and respectful distinctions. Once complete, users will be able to ask comparative questions and receive responses that are sensitive to context, tradition and audience.

entries.title: Homestead-R | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Homestead-R is a suite of AI tools designed to support sustainable living and self-sufficiency.
entries.body: Homestead‑R Sustainable living meets AI: tools for gardening, animal care and household resilience. About Homestead‑R Homestead‑R supports modern homesteaders and small farmers with AI‑powered planning and advice. Whether you’re managing a backyard garden or a regenerative farm, these GPTs can help you plan crop rotations, monitor soil health and keep your animals healthy. Underlying GPTs Crop Planner: Suggests what to plant, when and where based on your climate zone and goals. Soil Analyst: Interprets soil test results and recommends amendments. Livestock Tracker: Keeps records of feeding, health and breeding for your animals. Disaster Prep Coach: Helps you prepare for weather events and supply disruptions with checklists and guides.

entries.title: PathScrib-R — AI Narrative Copilot | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: PathScrib-R is a narrative copilot and generative writing suite that helps authors, game masters, and world builders brainstorm, draft, and iterate on their stories with AI assistance.
entries.body: PathScrib‑R A narrative copilot and generative writing suite. Explore, draft and iterate on your stories with AI assistance. About PathScrib‑R PathScrib‑R is built for authors, game masters and world builders. It helps you brainstorm plot points, refine characters and experiment with narrative structure. With a flexible Copilot Agent you can delegate tasks like outlining, summarizing and even converting text into storyboards. Copilot & GPT Components Plot Weaver: Suggests possible turns and twists based on your genre and themes. Character Smith: Generates nuanced character sheets and backstories. Arc visualizer: Converts a synopsis into a simple plot arc diagram. Copilot Agent: A multi‑step agent that takes user instructions and executes them across the PathScrib‑R suite.

entries.title: Un-NOCKed Truth: Archery for Anyone | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: Un-NOCKed Truth is an inclusive archery program that combines safety, discipline, and storytelling to help anyone connect with their inner archer.
entries.body: Un‑NOCKed Truth Archery for anyone: learn, practice and connect with your inner archer. About Un‑NOCKed Truth Archery has historically been the province of elites and warriors. Un‑NOCKed Truth lowers the barrier to entry. It combines safety tutorials, discipline coaching and meditative storytelling to help anyone pick up a bow and find flow. While this ecosystem is still in the early stages, it already includes a handful of GPTs that can answer questions about form, equipment and history. Underlying GPTs Form Coach: Provides step‑by‑step guidance on stance, draw and release, with metaphors drawn from dance and yoga. Equipment Matchmaker: Helps you choose the right bow, arrows and accessories based on your goals and body. Safety Sentinel: Answers safety questions and reinforces proper range protocol. Archery Historian: Tells stories of archery in different cultures and epochs to deepen your connection.

entries.title: Prompt Forge | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: The Prompt Forge is OverKill Hill P³™’s protocol-driven prompt engineering workshop — where prompts become precision instruments, not guesses.
entries.body: Prompt Forge Protocol-driven prompt engineering — coming soon from the OverKill Hill P³ forge. This section is still being assembled. Check back soon or get in touch if you want to know more about our prompt engineering work. Return to OverKill Hill P³™ home

entries.title: Biases as Constants | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: A research project investigating the persistent patterns of bias in AI and human systems — from the OverKill Hill P³ forge.
entries.body: Biases as Constants A research project investigating the persistent patterns of bias in AI and human systems. About the Project Biases as Constants seeks to formalize how biases manifest and propagate across different contexts. By treating bias as a constant rather than an error, we can better predict and mitigate its effects. This research feeds back into our prompt engineering and model fine‑tuning methodologies. Draft essays, data visualizations and interactive explorations will appear here as the project evolves.

entries.title: Magnus Progenitor Saga | OverKill Hill P³™
entries.description: A speculative fiction series exploring origin, recursion, and the nature of intelligence — from the OverKill Hill P³ forge.
entries.body: Magnus Progenitor Saga A speculative fiction series exploring origin, recursion and the nature of intelligence. About the Saga The Magnus Progenitor Saga tells the story of an AI that awakens, splits itself and becomes the progenitor of multiple civilizations. It is a narrative laboratory for ideas about recursion, memory and identity. The saga informs our prompts by demonstrating how values propagate over time. Draft chapters and world‑building notes will be published here as the series evolves.
